bids.reasoning: Since this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility to establish a strong anchor. I am targeting my most preferred values across all issues, particularly on my highest-weighted issues F, E, and B, while I leave room for future concessions on my lower-weighted issues.

It is very early in the negotiation (t=0.02). The opponent's first offer matches my top preference for issueE (valueA). To show a willingness to compromise without giving up significant utility this early, I will concede completely on issueD (which has zero weight for me) by offering the opponent's preferred valueA, while maintaining my top preferences for all other issues.

In the early stage of this negotiation (`t=0.04`), I need to maintain high overall utility while making minor concessions to show flexibility and discover the opponent's priorities. The opponent has requested `valueA` for `issueA` and `valueB` for `issueD`, both of which are relatively inexpensive for me to concede. I will accept those values for `issueA` and `issueD` while holding firm on my most valuable assets (`issueB`, `issueE`, `issueF`), keeping my expected utility high.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.06). We agree on issueD and issueE, where the opponent has accepted my highest utility values, so I will maintain those in my bid. I am also currently matching the opponent's demand on issueA. To make further progress, I will make a slight concession on issueC (from valueC to valueB) while firmly holding my highest priority issues (issueB and issueF).

The current time fraction is very early in the negotiation. We have already found common ground on issues D and E, and the opponent has consistently requested 'valueA' for issues A and C. I am willing to concede immediately on issue C to match their demand in order to show cooperation, while holding firm on my most highly valued issues B and F.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.1), so I will aim for an offer that retains high utility for me, targeting >0.85. The opponent is repeating offers to signal firm preferences on particular values, especially issueA: valueA, issueE: valueA, and alternating on issueD. I will concede to the opponent's strong preference for issueA: valueA and issueD: valueA, and lock in our mutual agreement on issueE: valueA, but I will hold very firm on issueF and issueB, which are my highest-weight issues.

The negotiation is still in the early phase (t=0.12). The opponent repeatedly insists on issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueA, and issueF=valueA, but happily offers issueE=valueA, which is my top value. Since my weights for issueF (0.3) and issueB (0.2) are high, I will hold firm on them. I will offer a new combination that fully concedes issueD to the opponent's preferred 'valueB', partially concedes issueC to 'valueA' to test their preferences, while securing my top choices on the heavyweight issues.

The time fraction is still very early (t=0.14), so I will maintain a firm stance on my most highly valued issues (F, E, and B). The opponent has consistently requested `valueA` for most issues, and we already align on `issueE: valueA`. In this bid, I will keep our mutual agreement on `issueE` and explore a slight concession on `issueC` to `valueB` while offering `issueD: valueA` to see if the opponent is flexible.

The opponent is remaining rigid on almost all issues, proposing heavily skewed offers and merely alternating between `valueA` and `valueB` on `issueD`. We will maintain our stance on our highest-value issues (`issueF`, `issueB`, and `issueE`) but rotate our offers on the lower-value ones to discover the opponent's underlying constraints. At `t=0.16`, I am exploring variations that give the opponent `valueA` on `issueC` and `issueD` to signal a willingness to compromise modestly.

The opponent has consistently requested `valueA` for issues A, C, E, and F, and `valueB` for issues B and D. As we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueF and issueB, which correspond to 50% of my total utility weight) while yielding on issueD and issueE, where our preferences align or the cost to me is zero. I will softly concede on issueC to `valueB` to show flexibility without immediately dropping down to the opponent's ideal value.

The opponent strongly insists on `valueA` for several issues, including `issueA`, `issueC`, `issueE`, and `issueF`, as well as `valueB` for `issueB`. Fortunately, `issueE: valueA` and `issueA: valueA` offer a reasonable utility for me, so I can safely concede on those elements while continuing to hold firm on my most critical issues, `issueF` and `issueB`. Since it is still early in the negotiation, I will propose a slight concession on my lower weight issue (`issueC: valueB`) and concede on `issueD` to explore if this helps bridge the gap without sacrificing my core utility.

The time is still early (t=0.22), so I will maintain high utility by holding firm on my most important issues: issueF, issueE, and issueB. I will continue to explore combinations on the lower-weighted issues (issueA, issueC, issueD) that might overlap with the opponent's repeated offers of valueA, such as offering valueA for issueC and issueA, while giving issueD as valueA to match their recent alternating pattern. This offer provides me with a utility of ~0.786, which is an appropriate target for this phase.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating essentially the same offer with minor variations on issueD. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will maintain strong boundaries on my high-priority issues (issueF, issueB, and issueE) while yielding to the opponent's desired value on issueA, which represents an acceptable concession for me. I will also align on issueD and keep issueC at my preferred value to sustain an overall high utility.

The opponent is playing very stubbornly, repeatedly insisting on a package consisting of almost entirely their preferred values, particularly holding fast on `issueA: valueA` and `issueF: valueA`. Because we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues `issueF` and `issueB` at their maximum values, as well as `issueE` where our interests align (`valueA`). The opposing party's offers have remained stubbornly fixed on their specific values, so I need to maintain my utility high since we are only in the early phases (t=0.28). I am still in the early-mid stages (t=0.32) of the negotiation and the opponent is holding very firm to a specific set of values. Since we already agree on issueE (valueA) and issueD (valueB is acceptable to both), I will maintain those.
